docs.text: OptiHeat Project Welcome to the OptiHeat Project website. OptiHeat is a collaboration between the Univerity of Bristol and Bristol City Council, with the aim of supporting social housing providers that want to deliver heat pumps into their housing stock. During the Spring of 2026 we will be running a series of event across the UK. This website also contains a repository of the resources that we have found and produced, all of which are free to download. Heatpump information for social housing providers Across the UK housing providers are making the switch the heatpumps. As part of the OptiHeat project we have created resources to support this transition. Supporting the Process We have broken out resources down into four main steps, that reflect the steps that housing provider has to go to when installing heatpumps. These are: Pre Install Design Installation Post Install This website is also structred in this manner to help you find the resources that are most relevent to you. Disclaimer This project is best endevours. We have tried to produce material that will be useful, but we cannot take any responsbility for decisions that are made based on the information given here. This is a fast moving field, and although we will do our best to keep resources up to date, we cannot make any guarantees on this.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: OptiHeat Workshops As part of the OptiHeat project we are running a nation wide series of workshops. During these workshops we will describe some of the challenges that the OptiHeat team have identified while delivering heat pumps into social housing. We will also go through some of the practical reources that exist to support others who are going through the same process. Links to these resources can be found on the Resouces page of this side. Upcoming Events Date Location Booking Link Tuesday 28 April 2026 Taunton Eventbrite link available soon Thursday 30th April 2026 Liverpool / Manchester Eventbrite link available soon Past Events Some events have already taken place, but don't worry, as some of these have been recorded. These recordings will be available for download here soon. Date Location Resources Tuesday 31 March 2026 Stoke-on-Trent Recording available soon

docs.title: Tools to help

docs.text: Heat Pump Manurfacturers Remote Assistance We believe that heat pump remote monitoring (which we prefer to call "remote assistance") has a significant role to play in improving the delivery of heat pumps across social housing. We believe it can contribute to improving the tenant's experience of the heat pump, while also saving social landlords money by reducing call outs. For this reason we have been carrying out a review of the different remote assistance technologies provided by heat pump manurfactuers. This has been collated to produce the remote assistance disemination material that can be found on our Resources page. We have tried to contact as many heat pump manurfacturers (and third-party heat-pump remote-monitoring compainies) as we can, but we are aware that there are many others out there that we have not yet been able to talk to. If you are a heat pump manurfacturer and would be like to contriubute to this project by getting your remote assistance offering included in our material, then please complete the webform linked to below. We will then get in touch to arrange an interview. Remote Assistance Online Form Thanks,
docs.title: Manufacturers

docs.text: We believe that heat pump remote monitoring (which we prefer to call "remote assistance") has a significant role to play in improving the delivery of heat pumps across social housing. We believe it can contribute to improving the tenant's experience of the heat pump, while also saving social landlords money by reducing call outs. For this reason we have been carrying out a review of the different remote assistance technologies provided by heat pump manurfactuers. This has been collated to produce the remote assistance disemination material that can be found on our Resources page. We have tried to contact as many heat pump manurfacturers (and third-party heat-pump remote-monitoring compainies) as we can, but we are aware that there are many others out there that we have not yet been able to talk to. If you are a heat pump manurfacturer and would be like to contriubute to this project by getting your remote assistance offering included in our material, then please complete the webform linked to below. We will then get in touch to arrange an interview. Remote Assistance Online Form Thanks,

docs.title: Tools to help

docs.text: Resources All the resources shown here are free to download. Strategic Review Social Housing provider Heat Pump Strategic Review template - Uploaded 31st March 2026 The purpose of the document is to provide a template that social housing provider officers might use to coordinate the review of activities within their organisations around heat pumps. This document has been produced by Bristol City Council who take no liability for it’s use. Quality Assurance BCC INTERNAL Air Source Heat Pump Guidance for officers and installers - Uploaded 1st April 2026 This document is intended as a guide to heat pump installation. It makes reference to but is not a substitution for industry standards set by bodies including MCS and the certification bodies, planning regulations and DNO procedures. It goes beyond some of the requirements of these regulations to ensure quality installations in Bristol. National Trust ASHP specification - Uploaded 31st March 2026 This Document describes the performance requirements for small scale Air Source Heat Pumps for the National Trust Let Estate. Broadacres ASHP specification - Uploaded 31st March 2026 This Document describes the specification requirements for Air Source Heat Pumps for the Broadacres Housing Assosiation. Social housing heat pump installation audit form - External Link This template audit form has been developed by Bristol City Council and Nesta to provide a clear process for auditing and quality assuring heat pumps installations in social housing. If you would like to repurpose this form, please read the instruction provided below. Social housing heat pump installation audit form instruction - External Link These are the instructions on how to use the template audit form provided above. ASHP Heat pump technical audit form - Uploaded 31st March 2026 This audit form was developed by Bristol City Council and Nesta to provide a clear process for auditing and quality assuring heat pumps installations in social housing. BCC heat pump repairs form - Uploaded 31st March 2026 This is a form to be used to record the outcomes of repair visits. BCC ASHP post installation 5 point check visit form - Uploaded 31st March 2026 This is a guide designed for non-technical staff when checking a heat pump installation. Heat Pump QA process map and metrics - Uploaded 31st March 2026 Very high level slides showing the process to heat pump installation and the metrics by which this process can be measured. Heat Pump QA process map and metrics data log - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A table for recording the heat pump process metrics. Post audit reporting protocol - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A document describing the protocol to carry out after a heat pump audit has raised issues. Training HVAC Education Hub - External Link A series of videos, delivered by Mario Dodić, giving a significant amount of information about heat pumps and how they should be designed, installed, controlled and maintained. Heat Geek - External Link A series of videos providing information about heat pumps. Handover Guidance CSE Heat Pump Advice leaflet - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A well produced leaflet, created by the Centre for Sustainable Energy, providing information about heatpumps. BCC CSE Tenant Advice Service heat pump advice for tenants - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A well produced leaflet providing information about heatpumps designed to be provided to tenants after they have had a heat pump installed. C3 Tenant ASHP short Handover document Nesta.docx - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A short document to give to tenants explaining how they can get the bets results from their heat pump. Set-by-step guides to tame and train your heat pump - External Link Jargon free pictorial guides taking you step by step through how to train and tame your heat pump. Remote Assistance Heat pump remote assistance privacy notice - Uploaded 31st March 2026 An example privacy notice conserning the data gathered by a heat pump remote assistance system. THIS IS TO BE USED AS AN EXAMPLE ONLY, AND SHOULD NOT BE SHARED WITH TENANTS UNTIL IT HAS BEEN APPROVED BY YOUR IN HOUSE LEGAL TEAM! Heat Pump Remote Assistance Disemination Material - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A guide to heat pump remote assistance systems and how they can benefit social housing landlords. Remote Assistance Manurfacturer Survey Results - Uploaded 31st March 2026 A spreadsheet contining the results of the survery into different manufacturers heat pump remote assistance offerings. This will be added to as more manurfacturers approve content for disemination. General Guidance MCS Domestic heat pumps - a best practice guide - External Link The standards and tools library provided by the MCS. Renewable Heating Hub - External Link A series of video providing information for home owners on the subject of heat pumps, sollar panels and house batteries. Planning for heat: what you need to know about heat pumps - External Link A webinar delivered by Nesta on the subject of heat pumps.
docs.title: Resources
